title: stipa pennata subvar. pygmaea
intro: Stipa pennata subvar. pygmaea, also known as dwarf feather grass, is a type of ornamental grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, and is known for its small, feathery plumes and delicate foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's aerial parts are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
